raw_review_excerpt: Current-artifact-first review passes on the requested focus. The plan now explicitly requires stale-token rejection symmetry on both approve and reject paths for both CLI and API, and it carries matching Phase 99 verify commands, exit criteria, evidence expectations, and prior-round closure notes. Coverage is slice-only: I reviewed the supplied plan text and prior-round claim packet, not the full campaign or directly inspected repo files.
closure_verification: The carried prior-round concern about reject-path symmetry is resolved in the current artifact.

prior_round_closure.title: Symmetric stale-token rejection proof
prior_round_closure.caller_claim: The plan was updated to add symmetric CLI and API reject-path stale-token verify commands and to tighten exit/evidence language so both approve and reject are explicitly proven.
prior_round_closure.verification_notes: Verified in the current artifact: CLI P99-T1 includes both approve and reject stale-token test commands; API P99-T2 includes both approve and reject stale-token test commands; both exit-criteria sections require proof on both paths; Cycle 5 records the same revision.

review.closure_verification: The carried prior-round concern about reject-path symmetry is resolved in the current artifact.
workflow_compliance: The plan is freeze-ready on the requested focus because it now states explicit verify commands, exit criteria, and evidence for symmetric stale-token rejection on approve and reject.
Exit criteria and verify commands are materially aligned with the stated acceptance criteria for the stale-gate safety requirement.
technical_reality: Within the current artifact, stale-gate protection is defined as optional-token validation in v1 and is required to fail clearly when used.
The plan now treats approve and reject symmetrically at both CLI and API surfaces for stale-token rejection proof.
assumptions_to_verify: The cited CLI and server test files still exist and remain the correct test homes at implementation time.
Current approve/reject routes and CLI paths can accept an expected-gate-token style parameter without conflicting with existing command contracts.
verification_sufficiency: Sufficient to judge the current plan artifact on the requested focus.
Not sufficient to certify full repo-code alignment or full-campaign closure because this review did not inspect repo files directly.
downstream_risks: If repo reality has drifted since the artifact's own preflight, the frozen verify commands may need mechanical adjustment even though the plan logic is sound.
review.freeze_recommendation: Safe to freeze. No blocker-level issues were identified.
